DG_DESeq2_Fibrinogen_And_Haptoglobin

Slizovskiy Lab

2026-01-23

Load libraries, phyloseq object, metadata

# Load Required Packages
library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(magrittr)
library(tibble)
library(microbiome)
library(ANCOMBC) # v2.8.1
library(lme4)
library(DESeq2)
library(EnhancedVolcano)
library(ggplot2)
library(pheatmap)
library(patchwork)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)


path.rds <- "G:/Shared drives/Microbiome_Inflammation_Study/GABRIELA_DATA_FILES/Cow_Inflammation_Study"
path.both <- file.path(path.rds, "DESeq2_Both/")
path.figures <- file.path(path.both, "Figures/")
# Load phyloseq
ps <- readRDS(file.path(path.rds, "ps.st.decontam.rds"))
ps_feces <- subset_samples(ps, Sample_Type == "Feces")
ps_feces
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21382 taxa and 142 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 142 samples by 57 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21382 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
saveRDS(ps_feces, file.path(path.rds, "ps.st.decontam.feces.rds"))
ps <- ps_feces
ps
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21382 taxa and 142 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 142 samples by 57 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21382 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Update metadata ===================================
metadata <- read.csv(file.path(path.rds, "Updated_Cow_Metadata.csv"), 
                     stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
ps_samples <- sample_names(ps)
metadata <- metadata[metadata$Sample_ID %in% ps_samples, ]
rownames(metadata) <- metadata$Sample_ID
metadata$Sample_ID <- NULL
sample_data(ps) <- sample_data(metadata) # Assign metadata to the phyloseq obj

# Rename ASVs  ========================================
rename_asvs <- function(ps) {
  old_names <- taxa_names(ps)
  new_names <- paste0("ASV", seq_along(old_names))
  
  # Convert taxonomy to data frame
  tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps))
  
  # Remove existing Sequence column if it exists
  if ("Sequence" %in% colnames(tax_df)) {
    tax_df <- tax_df[, !colnames(tax_df) %in% "Sequence", drop = FALSE]
  }
  
  # Add Sequence column at the beginning
  tax_df <- cbind(Sequence = old_names, tax_df)
  
  # Reassign to phyloseq object
  tax_table(ps) <- as.matrix(tax_df)
  taxa_names(ps) <- new_names
  
  return(ps)
}
ps <- rename_asvs(ps)
meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps)) # Metadata after renaming

# Modify metadata  ===================================
meta$ExtractionDate <- as.Date(meta$ExtractionDate, format = "%m/%d/%Y")
meta$ExtractionDate_cat <- factor(meta$ExtractionDate)

meta$Parity_numeric <- as.numeric(as.character(meta$Parity))
meta$Parity_Group <- factor(ifelse(meta$Parity_numeric <= 3,"Group1", "Group2"))

meta$BCS <- as.numeric(as.character(meta$BCS))
meta$BCS_Group <- factor(cut(meta$BCS,
                             breaks = c(-Inf, 2.5, 3.5, Inf),
                             labels = c("Low", "Medium", "High"),
                             right = TRUE))

meta$Fibrinogen_InflamStat <- factor(meta$Fibrinogen_InflamStat, 
                                     levels = c("Normal", "Elevated"))
meta$Fibrinogen_InflamStat<- relevel(meta$Fibrinogen_InflamStat, ref = "Normal")


meta$Haptoglobin_InflamStat <- factor(meta$Haptoglobin_InflamStat, levels = c("Normal", "Elevated"))
meta$Haptoglobin_InflamStat <- relevel(meta$Haptoglobin_InflamStat, ref = "Normal")

# Create Haptoglobin Mean column
if (all(c("Haptoglobin_1DIM", "Haptoglobin_3DIM") %in% colnames(meta))) {
  meta$Haptoglobin_Mean <- rowMeans(
    meta[, c("Haptoglobin_1DIM", "Haptoglobin_3DIM")],
    na.rm = TRUE
  )} else {
  stop("No Haptoglobin columns found in metadata")
}

# Make numeric
meta$Haptoglobin_Mean <- as.numeric(meta$Haptoglobin_Mean)

# Re-assign metadata to the phyloseq object  ===========
sample_data(ps) <- sample_data(meta)
dim(sample_data(ps))
## [1] 142  70
# OTUs ==============================================
if (taxa_are_rows(ps)) {
  otu <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix")   # already taxa x samples
} else {
  otu <- t(as(otu_table(ps), "matrix"))
}

# Align samples between counts and metadata
meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps))
common_samples <- intersect(colnames(otu), rownames(meta))
otu  <- otu[, common_samples]
meta <- meta[common_samples, ]

# Check dimensions
cat("Samples in counts:", ncol(otu), "\n")
## Samples in counts: 142
cat("Samples in metadata:", nrow(meta), "\n")
## Samples in metadata: 142

DESeq2 handling zeros

dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = otu,
  colData   = meta,
  design    = ~ Haptoglobin_InflamStat + Fibrinogen_InflamStat + BCS_Group + Parity_Group + ExtractionDate_cat)
## converting counts to integer mode
##   Note: levels of factors in the design contain characters other than
##   letters, numbers, '_' and '.'. It is recommended (but not required) to use
##   only letters, numbers, and delimiters '_' or '.', as these are safe characters
##   for column names in R. [This is a message, not a warning or an error]
dds
## class: DESeqDataSet 
## dim: 21382 142 
## metadata(1): version
## assays(1): counts
## rownames(21382): ASV1 ASV2 ... ASV21381 ASV21382
## rowData names(0):
## colnames(142): 1165D1LM 1165D3LM ... 9967D1LM 9967D3LM
## colData names(70): Corrected_Sample_ID Cow_ID ... BCS_Group
##   Haptoglobin_Mean
dds$meta <- relevel(dds$Fibrinogen_InflamStat, ref = "Normal")
dds$meta <- relevel(dds$Haptoglobin_InflamStat, ref = "Normal")
dds <- estimateSizeFactors(dds, type = "poscounts")
##   Note: levels of factors in the design contain characters other than
##   letters, numbers, '_' and '.'. It is recommended (but not required) to use
##   only letters, numbers, and delimiters '_' or '.', as these are safe characters
##   for column names in R. [This is a message, not a warning or an error]
print("Size factors calculated:")
## [1] "Size factors calculated:"
head(sizeFactors(dds))
##  1165D1LM  1165D3LM  1179D1LM  1179D3LM  1217D1LM  1217D3LM 
## 1.2373937 1.1975355 1.5200475 1.0464501 1.3172828 0.8031729

Run DESeq2

#dds <- DESeq(dds)
#saveRDS(dds, file.path(path.rds, "DeSeq2_Both/Files/DESeq_dds_Fibrino.rds"))
dds <- readRDS(file.path(path.rds, "DeSeq2_Both/Files/DESeq_dds_Fibrino.rds"))
dds
## class: DESeqDataSet 
## dim: 21382 142 
## metadata(1): version
## assays(6): counts mu ... replaceCounts replaceCooks
## rownames(21382): ASV1 ASV2 ... ASV21381 ASV21382
## rowData names(59): baseMean baseVar ... maxCooks replace
## colnames(142): 1165D1LM 1165D3LM ... 9967D1LM 9967D3LM
## colData names(72): Corrected_Sample_ID Cow_ID ... sizeFactor
##   replaceable
# Dispersion estimation
# dds_both_dispersion <- estimateDispersions(dds)
# saveRDS(dds_both_dispersion, file.path(path.rds, "DeSeq2_Both/Files/dds_both_dispersion.rds"))
dds_both_dispersion <- readRDS(file.path(path.rds, "DeSeq2_Both/Files/dds_both_dispersion.rds"))
plotDispEsts(dds)

Extract results:

# Get results (change contrast to your comparison)
alpha <- 0.01
resultsNames(dds)
##  [1] "Intercept"                                  
##  [2] "Haptoglobin_InflamStat_Elevated_vs_Normal"  
##  [3] "Fibrinogen_InflamStat_Elevated_vs_Normal"   
##  [4] "BCS_Group_Medium_vs_Low"                    
##  [5] "BCS_Group_High_vs_Low"                      
##  [6] "Parity_Group_Group2_vs_Group1"              
##  [7] "ExtractionDate_cat_2024.12.27_vs_2024.12.24"
##  [8] "ExtractionDate_cat_2025.01.18_vs_2024.12.24"
##  [9] "ExtractionDate_cat_2025.01.25_vs_2024.12.24"
## [10] "ExtractionDate_cat_2025.02.07_vs_2024.12.24"
## [11] "ExtractionDate_cat_2025.02.08_vs_2024.12.24"
res<- results(dds, alpha = alpha)
summary(res)
## 
## out of 6087 with nonzero total read count
## adjusted p-value < 0.01
## LFC > 0 (up)       : 335, 5.5%
## LFC < 0 (down)     : 799, 13%
## outliers [1]       : 2449, 40%
## low counts [2]     : 129, 2.1%
## (mean count < 0)
## [1] see 'cooksCutoff' argument of ?results
## [2] see 'independentFiltering' argument of ?results
# Make dataframe with taxonomy
res_df <- as.data.frame(res) %>%
  rownames_to_column("ASV") %>%
  filter(!is.na(padj)) %>%
  arrange(padj)

# Add taxonomy
tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps)) %>%
  rownames_to_column("ASV")
res_df <- left_join(res_df, tax_df, by = "ASV")
# res_df <- res_df %>% select(-ends_with(".x"), -ends_with(".y"))
res_df <- res_df %>%  mutate(Genus = ifelse(is.na(Genus) | Genus == "",  paste0("Uncharacterized:", Family), Genus))


# Significant results
sig_res <- filter(res_df, padj < 0.01)
sig_res <- sig_res[rowSums(otu[sig_res$ASV, ]) > 0, ]  # Remove zero-sum taxa

print(paste("Significant taxa:", nrow(sig_res)))
## [1] "Significant taxa: 1134"
# Sort by p-value:
sig_res_sorted <- sig_res[order(sig_res$padj, -abs(sig_res$log2FoldChange)), ]

# Check
head(sig_res_sorted, 20)
summary(sig_res_sorted)
##      ASV               baseMean        log2FoldChange       lfcSE       
##  Length:1134        Min.   :   1.366   Min.   :-30.00   Min.   :0.3874  
##  Class :character   1st Qu.:   2.438   1st Qu.:-25.41   1st Qu.:5.0776  
##  Mode  :character   Median :   4.802   Median :-22.08   Median :5.0838  
##                     Mean   :  18.582   Mean   :-10.67   Mean   :4.9000  
##                     3rd Qu.:  21.058   3rd Qu.: 17.39   3rd Qu.:5.0845  
##                     Max.   :1667.924   Max.   : 30.00   Max.   :5.0867  
##       stat             pvalue               padj             Sequence        
##  Min.   :-11.554   Min.   :0.000e+00   Min.   :0.000e+00   Length:1134       
##  1st Qu.: -5.049   1st Qu.:2.137e-07   1st Qu.:2.600e-06   Class :character  
##  Median : -4.344   Median :8.011e-06   Median :5.135e-05   Mode  :character  
##  Mean   : -2.152   Mean   :1.719e-04   Mean   :5.845e-04                     
##  3rd Qu.:  3.619   3rd Qu.:2.250e-05   3rd Qu.:9.619e-05                     
##  Max.   : 11.405   Max.   :3.115e-03   Max.   :9.994e-03                     
##    Kingdom             Phylum             Class              Order          
##  Length:1134        Length:1134        Length:1134        Length:1134       
##  Class :character   Class :character   Class :character   Class :character  
##  Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character  
##                                                                             
##                                                                             
##                                                                             
##     Family             Genus             Species         
##  Length:1134        Length:1134        Length:1134       
##  Class :character   Class :character   Class :character  
##  Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character  
##                                                          
##                                                          
## 
# Save
write.csv(sig_res_sorted,
  file.path(path.rds, "DeSeq_Fibrinogen/Files/result_both_annotated_sorted.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE)

Normalization using qPCR number

# Make dataframe of significant ASVs
sig_asvs <- sig_res$ASV
otu_sig <- otu[sig_asvs, , drop = FALSE]

# Ensure same sample order
otu_sig <- otu_sig[, rownames(meta)]

# Extract qPCR 16S load
meta$qpcr_16s_copies_ul <- gsub(",", "", meta$qpcr_16s_copies_ul)
meta$qpcr_16s_copies_ul <- as.numeric(meta$qpcr_16s_copies_ul)
qpcr_16s <- meta$qpcr_16s_copies_ul
names(qpcr_16s) <- rownames(meta)
qpcr_16s <- qpcr_16s[colnames(otu_sig)]

# Normalize abundance by dividing by qPCR copies per ASV
normalized_abundance <- sweep(otu_sig, 2, qpcr_16s, FUN = "/")

Correlation between ASV abundance and both markers

# Regression between concentration and inflammation

# Ensure Fibrinogen is numeric
meta$FIBRINOGEN_MEAN <- as.numeric(meta$FIBRINOGEN_MEAN)
meta$Haptoglobin_Mean <- as.numeric(meta$Haptoglobin_Mean)

# Keep only samples with qPCR and fibrinogen data
keep_samples <- !is.na(meta$qpcr_16s_copies_ul) & !is.na(meta$FIBRINOGEN_MEAN)
meta <- meta[keep_samples, ]

# Spearman correlation with continuous fibrinogen
fibrino_cor_results <- lapply(rownames(normalized_abundance), function(asv) {
  
  x <- as.numeric(normalized_abundance[asv, ])
  y <- meta$FIBRINOGEN_MEAN
  
  res <- cor.test(x, y, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
  
  data.frame(
    ASV = asv,
    fb_Spearman_rho = unname(res$estimate),
    fb_p_value = res$p.value)})

# Spearman correlation with continuous Haptoglobin
Haptoglobin_cor_results <- lapply(rownames(normalized_abundance), function(asv) {
  
  x <- as.numeric(normalized_abundance[asv, ])
  y <- meta$Haptoglobin_Mean
  
  res <- cor.test(x, y, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
  
  data.frame(
    ASV = asv,
    hp_Spearman_rho = unname(res$estimate),
    hp_p_value = res$p.value)})

# Combine results into a data frame
fibrino_cor_df <- bind_rows(fibrino_cor_results)
Haptoglobin_cor_df <- bind_rows(Haptoglobin_cor_results )
both_cor_df <- fibrino_cor_df %>% inner_join(Haptoglobin_cor_df, by = "ASV")

# Add the DA results: Log2FoldChange
both_cor_df <- both_cor_df %>% left_join(
  res_df %>% select(ASV, log2FoldChange), by = "ASV")

# Create the label: Elevated or Normal according the Log2FoldChange
both_cor_df <- both_cor_df %>%
  mutate(
    Inflammation = case_when(
      log2FoldChange > 0 ~ "Elevated",
      log2FoldChange < 0 ~ "Normal",
      TRUE               ~ "Non_significant"))

# Significant correlations
sum(both_cor_df$fb_p_value < 0.01, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 8
sum(both_cor_df$hp_p_value < 0.01, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 9
Both_cor_annotated <- both_cor_df 

# Merge correlation
Both_cor_annotated <- Both_cor_annotated %>% left_join(res_df, by = "ASV")

# Save results
write.csv(
  Both_cor_annotated,file.path(path.both, "Files/Both_Spearman_DESeq_Annotated.csv"),row.names = FALSE)
# Identify ASVs of interest (example filter)
Both_cor_annotated <- read.csv(file.path(path.both,   "Files/Both_Spearman_DESeq_Annotated.csv"),header = TRUE)
  
Both_hits <- Both_cor_annotated %>%
  filter(
    (abs(fb_Spearman_rho) > 0.2 & fb_p_value < 0.01) |
    (abs(hp_Spearman_rho) > 0.2 & hp_p_value < 0.01)) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(hp_Spearman_rho)))

head(Both_hits)
## Get a small dataframe of Both_hits: only ASV, Spearman_rho, p_value
Both_cor_df <- as.data.frame(Both_hits) # Ensure base data.frame
Both_cor_df$fb_Spearman_rho <- as.numeric(Both_cor_df$fb_Spearman_rho)
Both_cor_df$hp_Spearman_rho <- as.numeric(Both_cor_df$hp_Spearman_rho)
Both_cor_df$fb_p_value     <- as.numeric(Both_cor_df$fb_p_value)
Both_cor_df$hp_p_value     <- as.numeric(Both_cor_df$hp_p_value)


# Top ASVs by absolute rho
top_hits <- Both_cor_df[order(-abs(Both_cor_df$fb_Spearman_rho)), ]
top_hits <- top_hits  %>%  mutate(ASV_label = paste0(ASV, " (", Genus, ")"))
top_hits <- top_hits %>%
  mutate(
    Significant = case_when(
      fb_p_value < 0.01 & hp_p_value < 0.01 ~ "both_significant",
      fb_p_value < 0.01 ~ "Fibrinogen",
      hp_p_value < 0.01 ~ "Haptoglobin",
      TRUE             ~ "Non_significant"))

asvs_to_plot <- top_hits %>%  select(ASV, Genus, Significant, Inflammation) %>%  distinct()
asvs_to_plot <- asvs_to_plot[asvs_to_plot$ASV %in% rownames(normalized_abundance), ]
asvs_to_plot
# Save results
write.csv(
  asvs_to_plot,file.path(path.rds,
            "DeSeq2_Both/Files/Both_asvs_to_plot.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE)

Plots

Barplot

# barplot significant taxa by log2FoldChange ------------------------
barplot.df <- Both_cor_annotated %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(log2FoldChange.x))) %>%
  mutate(
    Taxa = reorder(ASV, log2FoldChange.x),
    Genus = ifelse(is.na(Genus) | Genus == "",  paste0("Uncharacterized:", Family), Genus))

label_asvs <- unique(asvs_to_plot$ASV)

y_labels <- setNames(
  ifelse(
    barplot.df$ASV %in% label_asvs,
    paste0(barplot.df$ASV, " (", barplot.df$Genus, ")"),
    ""),  barplot.df$ASV)


barplot <- ggplot(barplot.df, aes(x = log2FoldChange.x, y = Taxa)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = log2FoldChange.x), position = "identity") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red") +
  geom_text(
    data = dplyr::filter(barplot.df, ASV %in% label_asvs),
    aes(x = 0, y = Taxa, label = paste0("*", ASV, " (", Genus, ")")),
    hjust = 0,  vjust=0,   size = 3 ) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = y_labels) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = function(x) seq(
      from = floor(min(x, na.rm = TRUE) / 10) * 10,
      to   = ceiling(max(x, na.rm = TRUE) / 10) * 10,
      by   = 10)) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Differentially Abundant Taxa by Fibrinogen and Haptoglobin") +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(), 
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

barplot

Heatmap

order <- sig_res_sorted %>%
  arrange(desc(log2FoldChange)) %>% pull(ASV)

cor.estimate.ord <- Both_cor_annotated %>%
  filter(ASV %in% order) %>%   # only ASVs in barplot
  mutate(
    ASV = factor(ASV, levels = order),  # force the desired order
    is_hit = ASV %in% Both_hits$ASV,
    fb_sig = fb_p_value < 0.01,
    hp_sig = hp_p_value < 0.01
  ) %>%  arrange(ASV)

mat <- cor.estimate.ord %>%
  select(fb_Spearman_rho, hp_Spearman_rho) %>%
  as.matrix()

sig_mat <- cor.estimate.ord %>%
  select(fb_sig, hp_sig) %>%
  mutate(
    fb_sig = ifelse(fb_sig, "*", ""),
    hp_sig = ifelse(hp_sig, "*", "")
  ) %>%
  as.matrix()

rownames(mat)     <- as.character(cor.estimate.ord$ASV)
rownames(sig_mat) <- rownames(mat)
colnames(sig_mat) <- colnames(mat)

estimate.max.fb <- Both_cor_annotated %>% 
  filter(!is.na(fb_Spearman_rho)) %>% 
  .$fb_Spearman_rho %>% 
  abs() %>% 
  max() %>% 
  round(digits = 2)

estimate.max.hp <- Both_cor_annotated %>% 
  filter(!is.na(hp_Spearman_rho)) %>% 
  .$hp_Spearman_rho %>% 
  abs() %>% 
  max() %>% 
  round(digits = 2)

rho_lim <- max(estimate.max.fb, estimate.max.hp)
col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(-rho_lim, 0, rho_lim),c("blue", "white", "red"))

col_labels <- c("Fb", "Hp")

# Create a top annotation with the column names
top_ann <- HeatmapAnnotation(
  Biomarker = anno_text(col_labels, 
                        rot = 0,            # horizontal text
                        gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"))
)

# Heatmap
ht <- Heatmap(
  mat,
  name = "Spearman ρ",
  col = col_fun,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_columns = FALSE,
  show_column_names = FALSE,   # remove x labels
  show_row_names = FALSE,      # remove y labels
  top_annotation = top_ann,
  row_title = NULL,            # remove row title,
  cell_fun = function(j, i, x, y, width, height, fill) {

    if (sig_mat[i, j] == "*") {
      # draw star
      grid.text("*", x, y, gp = gpar(fontsize = 5, fontface = "bold"))
      # draw ASV name slightly to the right of the star
      grid.text(
        rownames(mat)[i],
        x + unit(0.6, "mm"),
        y,
        just = "left",
        gp = gpar(fontsize = 5)
      )
    }
  }
)
## Warning: You defined `cell_fun` for a heatmap with more than 100 rows or
## columns, which might be very slow to draw. Consider to use the
## vectorized version `layer_fun`.
ht

Combine Barplot and Heatmap

# Barplot + Heatmap
ht_grob <- grid.grabExpr(ComplexHeatmap::draw(
    ht, heatmap_legend_side = "bottom",
    padding = unit(c(0, 0, 0, 0), "mm")))

ht_plot <- wrap_elements(full = ht_grob)

final_plot <- barplot + ht_plot +
  plot_layout(widths = c(4, 1), guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

final_plot

ggsave(
  filename = "Fig_4_Both_DESeq2_Barplot_Heatmap.svg",
  plot = final_plot,  
  path = file.path(path.figures),
  width = 10,   height = 10,   dpi = 300 )

Individual Correlation Plots

# Function to get taxon label (Genus or Class)
get_tax_label <- function(asv) {
  g <- tax[asv, "Genus"]
  c <- tax[asv, "Class"]

  if (!is.na(g) && g != "") {
    return(g)} 
  else if (!is.na(c) && c != "") {return(paste0("Class: ", c))}
  else {return("Taxon: Unknown")}
}
#### Overlap Plots for Significant Correlation ASVs ------------------------
plot_list <- list()

for (i in seq_len(nrow(asvs_to_plot))) {

  asv <- asvs_to_plot$ASV[i]
  tax_label <- asvs_to_plot$Genus[i]

  if (is.na(tax_label) || tax_label == "") {
    tax_label <- "Uncharacterized"
  }

  # Prepare data (long format)
  df_plot <- data.frame(
    Abundance = as.numeric(normalized_abundance[asv, ]),
    Fibrinogen = meta$FIBRINOGEN_MEAN,
    Haptoglobin = meta$Haptoglobin_Mean
  )

  df_plot <- df_plot[df_plot$Abundance > 0, ]

  df_long <- tidyr::pivot_longer(
    df_plot,
    cols = c(Fibrinogen, Haptoglobin),
    names_to = "Marker",
    values_to = "Inflammation"
  )

  # correlation values
  rho_fb <- as.numeric(top_hits$fb_Spearman_rho[top_hits$ASV == asv][1])
  p_fb   <- as.numeric(top_hits$fb_p_value[top_hits$ASV == asv][1])

  rho_hp <- as.numeric(top_hits$hp_Spearman_rho[top_hits$ASV == asv][1])
  p_hp   <- as.numeric(top_hits$hp_p_value[top_hits$ASV == asv][1])

  label_text <- paste0(
    "FB: rho=", round(rho_fb, 3), ", p=", signif(p_fb, 3), "\n",
    "HP: rho=", round(rho_hp, 3), ", p=", signif(p_hp, 3)
  )

  p <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Inflammation, y = Abundance, color = Marker)) +
    geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.8) +
    labs(
      title = bquote(.(asv) ~ "(" * italic(.(tax_label)) * ")"),
      x = "Inflammation Marker",
      y = "Normalized Abundance") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::scientific) +
    annotate(
    "text",
    x = -Inf, y = Inf,
    label = label_text,
    hjust = -0.05, vjust = 1.2, size = 3.2) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.8),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )

  plot_list[[asv]] <- p

# Save
 ggsave(
    filename = file.path(path.figures, paste0("Correlation_", asv, ".svg")),
    plot = p, width = 6, height = 5,  dpi= 300  )}
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
#### Facet Plots for Significant Correlation ASVs ------------------------
plot_list <- list()

for (i in seq_len(nrow(asvs_to_plot))) {

  asv <- asvs_to_plot$ASV[i]
  tax_label <- asvs_to_plot$Genus[i]
  infl_status <- asvs_to_plot$Inflammation[asvs_to_plot$ASV == asv]

  df_plot <- data.frame(
    Abundance = as.numeric(normalized_abundance[asv, ]),
    Fibrinogen = meta$FIBRINOGEN_MEAN,
    Haptoglobin = meta$Haptoglobin_Mean)

  df_plot <- df_plot[df_plot$Abundance > 0, ]

  df_long <- tidyr::pivot_longer(
    df_plot,
    cols = c(Fibrinogen, Haptoglobin),
    names_to = "Marker",
    values_to = "Inflammation") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(InflammationStatus = factor(
      infl_status, levels = c("Normal", "Elevated")))

  # correlation values
  rho_fb <- as.numeric(top_hits$fb_Spearman_rho[top_hits$ASV == asv][1])
  p_fb   <- as.numeric(top_hits$fb_p_value[top_hits$ASV == asv][1])

  rho_hp <- as.numeric(top_hits$hp_Spearman_rho[top_hits$ASV == asv][1])
  p_hp   <- as.numeric(top_hits$hp_p_value[top_hits$ASV == asv][1])

  ann_df <- data.frame(
    Marker = c("Fibrinogen", "Haptoglobin"),
    label_text <- paste0(
    "Fb: p=" , signif(p_fb, 3), "| ρ=", round(rho_fb, 3), "\n",
    "Hp: p=", signif(p_hp, 3), "| ρ=", round(rho_hp, 3) ))
      

  p <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Inflammation, y = Abundance)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = InflammationStatus), size = 4, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.8, color = "black") +
    facet_wrap(~ Marker, scales = "free_x") +
    labs(
      title = bquote(.(asv) ~ "(" * italic(.(tax_label)) * ")"),
      x = "Inflammation Marker",
      y = "Normalized Abundance"  ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Elevated" = "red","Normal"   = "blue")) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::scientific) +
    annotate(
    "text",
    x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = label_text, hjust = -0.05, vjust = 1.2, size = 3.2) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.8),
      legend.position = "none")

  plot_list[[asv]] <- p
  
# Save
 ggsave(
    filename = file.path(path.figures, paste0("Facet_Correlation_", asv, ".svg")),
    plot = p, width = 6, height = 5,  dpi= 300  )}
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# Show the first ASV plot
for (i in 1:3) {print(plot_list[[i]])}
